validation_thesis: Test whether Fortune 500 procurement leaders will commit budget or a defined internal buying step for a $3,000 procurement leakage diagnostic before any product exists.
key_unknowns: Can the founder reach qualified Fortune 500 procurement buyers with only one weak second-degree intro plus targeted outbound?
Will buyers pay for a fixed-scope diagnostic without a software product?
Does one repeatable problem statement appear across early conversations?
Will prospects stay within a narrow wedge instead of asking for custom consulting?
motion: Weak warm intro plus targeted outbound to named enterprise buyers, ending in a paid diagnostic or defined commercial next step.

day_by_day_plan.objective: Define ICP, offer, and target list
actions: Build a list of 25 Fortune 500 target accounts
Identify 40 named buyers across procurement, procurement operations, and finance transformation
Prepare a one-page paid diagnostic offer with fixed scope and price

day_by_day_plan.objective: Launch intro request and first outbound batch
actions: Send the weak second-degree intro request with a forwardable note
Send 20 personalized emails
Send 10 LinkedIn messages
Ask 5 relevant contacts for referrals

day_by_day_plan.objective: Expand outreach and convert replies into calls
actions: Send 15 more personalized messages
Follow up on all prior outreach
Run any booked calls and end each with a commercial ask
numeric_targets: 100% of calls end with a commercial ask

day_by_day_plan.objective: Run early calls and test price and urgency
actions: Complete 2-3 qualified buyer calls
Probe for costly reporting gaps and current workaround pain
Ask one buyer whether $3,000 is low enough for a quick buy
numeric_targets: 2 buyers confirm a painful analytics gap

day_by_day_plan.objective: Reach conversation target and send proposals
actions: Complete remaining calls to reach 5-6 total
Send proposals to urgent prospects
Ask the strongest prospect for a paid diagnostic or scheduled internal buying step

day_by_day_plan.objective: Follow up and narrow the wedge if needed
actions: Follow up on open proposals
Narrow to one use case if feedback clusters
Disqualify unpaid custom consulting requests
numeric_targets: 1 narrowed wedge chosen if pattern appears

day_by_day_plan.objective: Make go, revise, or kill decision
actions: Score results against thresholds
Choose go, revise, or kill
Preserve call notes, objections, and buying-process evidence

success_criteria: Go: 5 or more qualified buyer conversations in 7 days
Go: 2 or more buyers confirm the same high-cost analytics problem
Go: 1 or more buyers agrees to a $3,000 paid diagnostic or a defined internal buying step within 30 days
Revise: 3-4 qualified conversations and real pain, but no acceptance of the current offer
kill_criteria: Fewer than 3 qualified conversations after 45 or more targeted touches plus the intro attempt
No buyer agrees to pay or advance a defined commercial step
Prospects mostly ask for free consulting or fully custom work
No repeated urgent problem emerges across the first 5-6 conversations
evidence_to_capture: Paid diagnostic acceptance or rejection reasons
